item: Everyone gets the same number regardless of role or seniority
Same salary within role, zero differentiation for experience
Salary "range" per role that's actually a €2K spread
Less than 35K (criminal tier)
Exactly 40K
More than 40K (unicorn square)
The raise is under inflation — so technically a pay cut
Raise expressed as a percentage that sounds generous but is €47/month
Notification comes by email, no meeting
Just the number, no actual contract attached
Notice arrives on the last business day of the month
Notice arrives "next week" = June
HR sends it at 6pm on a Friday
The email subject line is aggressively cheerful
You find out your number from a colleague before HR tells you
Contract doesn't specify role responsibilities
No career path or growth plan attached
Data Scientist title but the job description is clearly Data Engineering
Job title changed without discussion
Contract references a role level/grade nobody has ever heard of
Variable bonus mentioned but conditions are impossibly vague
No room for negotiation — "this is the final offer"
We'll revisit in 1 year — the increase is very low
No questions to HR = "doesn't exist"
You're told to "be grateful for the market conditions"
French colleagues react badly
Someone rage-quits in the group chat
Suspiciously cheerful person — they definitely got more than you
The meeting goes dead silent for 20 minutes
Someone asks "can we talk about this?" and gets ghosted
Immediate LinkedIn activity spike from half the team
Meal vouchers under €15
Meal vouchers at exactly €15 (the bare minimum)
But remember, you also get [benefit nobody asked for]
Remote work days presented as part of the compensation package
It's not about the money, it's about the project (said by someone clearly upset)
Group lunch turns into a therapy session
Someone pulls out a competing offer they've been sitting on
At least we have good work-life balance — said with dead eyes
Someone does the math on cost of living and posts it in Teams unprompted
Executives schedule a team morale meeting suspiciously close to salary day
Leadership sends a company-wide we value each of you email the same week
Event scheduled in the following days — clearly damage control
They use the term total compensation to make the number sound bigger
We fought hard for you with zero evidence of any fight
Ricard visibly uncomfortable, clearly just the messenger
Ricard gives the notice rather than Telina/HR
Someone receives the wrong person's salary letter
The Contract has a typo and HR sends a correction email
Gross vs net confusion — someone celebrates then does the math
Contract references a start date that's already passed
The PDF is clearly a recycled template with someone else's formatting artifacts
You get two different numbers from two different documents
Glassdoor reviews spike within 48 hours
Someone casually asks what the market rate is in a team meeting
Someone discovers the company is hiring their same role externally at a higher salary
